title: lobelia pumila
intro: Lobelia pumila, also known as water lobelia or dwarf lobelia, is a flowering plant species that belongs to the Lobelia genus. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, primarily due to the presence of lobeline, a compound that has been shown to have neurostimulant and expectorant effects. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, including relief from respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as potential anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties. Lobelia pumila is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and extracts, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it for medicinal purposes.
